annotations.content: Scene 08_We belong to all of creation
The day after survivors' day the police put razor wire around our burial ground, Turtle Island. As if they owned it.
It's us who belong to the land. We belong to the water. We belong to the air. We belong to all of creation. And it's not the other way around.
I'm from here. I'm born of the OCETI SAKOWIN people. But I know that I'm a guest here. We are all guests. We are all guests on Mother Earth - Unci Maka. We are all guests in this dream.

The Dakota access pipeline isn't the only snake there is. There's hundreds that are being proposed to drill across the United States. To put a pipeline in everyone's backyard. The horrifying vision of this country. This is just one of the battles that we all need to fight.

Tatanka Yotanka - Sitting Bull - was a prophet. One of the things that he said was that if a man looses something and goes back to look for it he'll find it. He's speaking to us in this day in how to reclaim our identity and to heal.
When you come to camp you are greated by security at the front gate saying "Welcome home!" Our camp is a place to relearn values we have lost. And that's why it's home.

I am not dreaming. I'm awake. I have been walking by the spirit inside that demanded I open my eyes and see the world around me. See that my children's future was in peril. See that my life couldn't wait in slumber anymore. See that I was honored to be amoung those who are awake. To be alive at this point in time. To see the rising of the Oceti Sakowin. To see the gathering of the nations and beyond that the gathering of all races and all faiths. Will you wake up and dream with us? Will you join and dream? Will you join us?
